name: Parc de Collserola, Can Borrell, Paseo de las aguas,  - circular
region_intro: Mountain biking in Serra de Collserola offers an extensive network of trails within a vast natural landscape, serving as a vital green space near Barcelona. This coastal mountain range features diverse ecosystems, including Aleppo pine and holm oak forests, with varied terrain suitable for different skill levels. The park's highest point is Tibidabo at 512 meters, providing significant elevation changes across its numerous paths. Cyclists can explore routes ranging from easier rides along the Llobregat to more challenging trails with substantial climbs and panoramic viewpoints. Best mountain bike trails in Serra De Collserola The most popular mountain bike trail is Parc de Collserola, Can Borrell, Paseo de las aguas, - circular, an 18.8 miles (30.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes sections of the popular Passeig de les Aigües, known for continuous climbs and scenic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vuelta de Collserola desde la estación Valldoreix, a difficult 42.4 miles (68.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gains and offers panoramic views of Barcelona and the surrounding areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Sant Medir and La Salut Hermitages Loop, a 19.7 miles (31.7 km) trail leading through historic hermitages and diverse forest landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Mountain biking in Serra De Collserola is defined by coastal mountain terrain, dense pine and holm oak forests, and numerous panoramic viewpoints. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging singletracks. The routes in Serra De Collserola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4200 reviews. More than 49000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Serra De Collserola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Serra de Collserola?
A: Serra de Collserola features a diverse coastal mountain terrain with significant elevation changes, as its highest point, Tibidabo, reaches 512 meters. You'll ride through dense Aleppo pine and holm oak forests, with trails ranging from smooth paths to more technical singletracks with rock gardens and loose rocks. The park's extensive network includes over 250 kilometers of paths where cycling is permitted.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Serra de Collserola?
A: Yes, Serra de Collserola offers trails for various skill levels, including options suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952549708/ruta-llobregat-entre-paseo-de-san-vicente-y-puente-peatonal-de-castelbisbal-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta Llobregat entre Paseo de San Vicente y puente peatonal de Castelbisbal - circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route with minimal elevation gain, perfect for those starting out or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking in Serra de Collserola?
A: The park is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can enjoy breathtaking panoramic views of Barcelona and the Mediterranean Sea from numerous 'miradors' (viewpoints), such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cypresses Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Medir Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1059270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Creu d'Olorda Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, which you can explore on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e933455634/sant-medir-and-la-salut-hermitages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Medir and La Salut Hermitages Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How accessible is Serra de Collserola by public transport for mountain bikers?
A: Serra de Collserola is highly accessible by public transport, with options available on all sides of the mountains and even through its center. Bike-friendly trains allow for flexible routes, making it easy to start and finish your ride without needing to complete a full loop back to your starting point.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Serra de Collserola?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Serra de Collserola are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937541279/parc-de-collserola-can-borrell-paseo-de-las-aguas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parc de Collserola, Can Borrell, Paseo de las aguas, - circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 30.2 km loop featuring continuous climbs and scenic views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Serra de Collserola?
A: The komoot community highly rates mountain biking in Serra de Collserola, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4200 reviews. Riders frequently praise the extensive network of trails, the diverse natural landscapes, and the stunning panoramic views of Barcelona. The ability to escape the city and connect with nature is also a common highlight.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Serra de Collserola?
A: While the park is a natural space, specific regulations regarding dogs on mountain bike trails can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other visitors. Always check local signage and park rules before heading out with your pet to ensure a safe and enjoyable experience for everyone.

Q: Are there any regulations or speed limits for mountain biking in Serra de Collserola?
A: Yes, there are regulations in place to ensure the safety and enjoyment of all park users. A speed limit of 20 km/h is enforced on trails. Additionally, biking is generally prohibited on single tracks (paths less than 3 meters wide), and pedestrians always have priority. It's important to respect these rules to preserve the natural environment and ensure a harmonious experience for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Serra de Collserola?
A: Serra de Collserola is enjoyable for mountain biking year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and lush scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are generally mild, making it a good option for escaping colder climates, though some trails might be damp after rain.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Serra de Collserola?
A: Absolutely. Serra de Collserola offers numerous challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. Trails near Casa Juanco, for instance, are known for their technical sections, including singletracks, rock gardens, and loose rocks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937615637/vuelta-de-collserola-desde-la-estacion-valldoreix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta de Collserola desde la estación Valldoreix&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult 68.3 km path with significant elevation gains, providing a demanding ride for advanced cyclists.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Serra de Collserola?
A: Serra de Collserola boasts an extensive network of mountain bike trails, with over 690 routes recorded on komoot. This includes a wide variety of options, with 94 easy, 333 moderate, and 270 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every skill level.
tips:
Can Borrell is a building in the municipality of Sant Cugat del Vallés (Vallès Occidental) that forms part of the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia. Inside, the date 1783 appears on a door.
Can Borrell is very popular with hikers and mountain bikers.
